_comment: What the HYPOTHESIZE subagent (vr-hypothesize) emits, and what FEEDBACK writes after DEEP. vr_hypo_merge.py integrates into out/hypotheses.json. ALWAYS produce a MIX of levels — not only 'concrete'. 'area' = broad exploratory direction (keeps search wide, prevents tunnel vision); 'mechanism' = a design mechanism to scrutinize; 'concrete' = a specific, DEEP-testable claim. no-safe-patterns rule: area/mechanism hypotheses can NEVER be refuted/closed as safe (the merge enforces this — a refute on them is downgraded to 'open'); only 'concrete' hypotheses can be confirmed/refuted.

hypotheses.why: queue lifetime spans ioctl + mmap + IRQ paths — complex ownership
head/tail updated without lock on fast path
tail index not re-validated against ring size before write at suspected L120
tail wraps but write uses stale tail copy

hypotheses.note: DEEP found re-validation at L122 (cited) — this specific claim disproven
tried to close the AREA — merge will KEEP it open per no-safe-patterns
